name: Czechoslovakia
note: Created from the Habsburg collapse at Versailles — the most successful democratic state in interwar Central Europe. Munich Agreement (1938) forced it to cede the Sudetenland to Hitler; fully occupied 1939. Liberated 1945; Communist coup 1948. The Prague Spring (1968) — Alexander Dubček's 'socialism with a human face' — was crushed by Soviet tanks. Velvet Revolution (1989) ended communism peacefully. Velvet Divorce (1993) split into Czech Republic and Slovakia.

figures.name: Tomáš Garrigue Masaryk
figures.significance: Philosopher-president who created Czechoslovakia through diplomacy at Versailles; a genuine liberal democrat who built Central Europe's most successful interwar state; his son Jan was defenestrated after the Communist coup

figures.name: Alexander Dubček
figures.significance: Led the Prague Spring — 'socialism with a human face'; removed by Soviets; worked as a forest ranger for 20 years; returned as Speaker of Parliament after the Velvet Revolution

figures.name: Václav Havel
figures.significance: Playwright turned dissident turned president; led the Velvet Revolution (1989); first post-communist President; the most admired European political figure of his generation
